title: YouTube Shorts Launches AI-Powered Deepfake Cloning for Creators
summary: YouTube Shorts is introducing an AI feature that allows creators to clone themselves realistically. This move highlights the platform's complex relationship with AI-generated content, balancing innovation with the challenges of deepfake scams and impersonations.
body:
YouTube Shorts is rolling out a new AI-powered feature that enables creators to realistically clone themselves on camera. The tool, hinted at earlier this year, reflects the platform's ongoing efforts to integrate generative AI while managing the pitfalls of AI-generated content. YouTube says the new feature will help creators produce more engaging content, but it also raises concerns about the proliferation of deepfake scams and impersonations.

The launch of this feature underscores the dual nature of AI in content creation. On one hand, it democratizes high-quality production, allowing creators to experiment with new forms of expression. On the other, it exacerbates existing challenges around authenticity and misinformation. YouTube's struggle to contain AI slop and deepfake scams is well-documented, and this new tool adds another layer of complexity to their moderation efforts.

As the feature rolls out, reactions from creators and viewers will be crucial. Some may embrace the creative possibilities, while others may express concerns about the ethical implications. YouTube will need to carefully monitor the use of this tool to prevent misuse. The broader question remains: can platforms successfully harness the power of AI without compromising trust and authenticity?
